title: diagram-editor widget params
description: An interactive commutative-diagram editor. Readers DRAG nodes to rearrange the layout (free / snap-to-grid) and TOGGLE individual arrows on/off; the widget re-evaluates declared `relations` (commutative squares, exact sequences) on every change and reports which paths are equal, which would-be paths fail, and where exactness is violated. Where `clickable-diagram` shows a fixed picture and `composition-explorer` walks a static composition table, this widget puts the diagram itself under the reader's hand: rearranging makes a square's commutativity visually obvious, toggling an arrow makes the consequence of removing it immediate. The driving IIFE is self-contained (no page-global library dependency) and theme-aware via a MutationObserver on `data-theme`. Both exports are pure functions of params; a React / SSR / any-frontend consumer can ignore renderScript and drive its own renderer from the schema alone (nodes + arrows + relations is the full data model).

widgetId.description: DOM id for the outer <div class="widget"> wrapper. All inner element ids derive from this so multiple instances coexist on a page.

title.description: Display title rendered inside .hd > .ttl. May contain $...$ LaTeX (rendered by the page's KaTeX auto-render pass).

hint.description: Optional short hint rendered inside .hd > .hint. Defaults to a generic gesture string when omitted.

width.description: SVG viewport width in user units. CSS layer caps display at 100% of container.

height.description: SVG viewport height in user units. Increase when many nodes / arrows would otherwise crowd.

palette.description: Project accent color used for selected/active arrows and the satisfied-relation glyph. Resolved at render time via var(--<palette>) so the page theme governs hue.

mode.description: Layout-edit mode. `free` lets nodes be dragged to any pixel; `snap-grid` snaps to a 6x4 grid (so a square stays square as the reader drags); `fixed` disables drag entirely (only the arrow toggles + relation evaluation remain).

nodes.description: Diagram objects. `x` / `y` are RATIOS in [0, 1] of the SVG viewport — width-independent so the same data renders sensibly at any size. `label` may carry $...$ LaTeX.

id.description: Unique node id within this widget instance.

x.description: Initial x position as a ratio of the viewBox width.

y.description: Initial y position as a ratio of the viewBox height.

color.description: Node label / outline accent. Resolved via var(--<color>) (or var(--ink) for `ink`).

arrows.description: Diagram morphisms. `from` / `to` reference node ids. `kind` controls the rendered glyph: `morphism` (plain →), `mono` (↪), `epi` (↠), `iso` (≅), `dotted` (dashed →, used for derived/induced morphisms). `togglable` arrows render with a small switch in the controls row; non-togglable arrows are always present.

id.description: Unique arrow id within this widget instance — referenced by `relations`.

from.description: Source node id (must reference an entry in `nodes`).

to.description: Target node id (must reference an entry in `nodes`).

label.description: Optional arrow label. KaTeX delimiters allowed.

kind.description: Arrow style: `morphism` plain, `mono` hooked tail (↪), `epi` double-headed (↠), `iso` ≅ glyph above, `dotted` dashed (induced).

curve.description: Curve offset perpendicular to the (from, to) line; positive curves outward (above for left-to-right). Useful when two arrows share endpoints (forward/back, or two parallel maps).

togglable.description: If true, the controls row gets a checkbox for this arrow; unchecking removes the arrow from the diagram and from any relation evaluation.

relations.description: Declared relations the widget evaluates on every drag-end / toggle. Each relation carries a `kind` and the relevant arrow / node ids. The widget reports each relation's status as ✓ (satisfied), ✗ (violated), or ? (some referenced arrow is toggled off — indeterminate).

lhs.description: Composition path (left-to-right read order: arrow ids applied in sequence). Both lhs and rhs must share start node and end node.

rhs.description: The other path. The relation says `lhs == rhs` as morphisms — when curated, the widget reports ✓ if all referenced arrows are present (commutativity is asserted, not solved); ✗ if endpoints disagree; ? if any arrow is toggled off.

label.description: Optional human-readable name (e.g. 'top-right square'). Defaults to a generated description.

lhs.description: Incoming chain (arrow ids), composing into node `at`.

rhs.description: Outgoing chain (arrow ids), composing out of node `at`. Exactness asserts image(lhs) = ker(rhs) — curated, reported ✓ when both chains are present and the diagram still resolves to `at`; ✗ if endpoints fail; ? if any arrow is missing.

at.description: Node id where the sequence is exact (the joint endpoint of lhs and start of rhs).

sectionComment.description: ARTIFACT. Optional banner comment preserved when migrating sources, emitted as `/* <sectionComment> */` between the leading <script> tag and the IIFE opener.
